todos.description: Register the real Genkit model/embedder identity instead of accepting LLMObs's generic `custom` fallback. Use the registered action name as the proven modelName and derive modelProvider only from explicitly supported, proven action-name prefixes; update tests to assert meaningful identity.

Implement provider ownership/demotion. Genkit currently always emits an `llm` span and token metrics even when an enabled supported provider LLMObs integration owns the underlying request. Follow the LangChain precedent: map proven provider prefixes, check the provider plugin, demote Genkit to `workflow`, and omit duplicate token/cost metrics; keep custom models as Genkit `llm` spans.

Resolve and test DD_TRACE_OTEL_ENABLED behavior. Exact-version runtime evidence produces a duplicate native Genkit OTel span, and that span carries raw `genkit:input`/`genkit:output`; the embedder case serializes its numeric vector into ordinary APM metadata. Establish one authoritative topology and prevent prompts/documents/vectors from leaking through the native duplicate before the live gate.

summary: Changes required. Default exact-version APM+LLMObs tests pass 18/18 and the Orchestrion/composite lifecycle is coherent, but model identity, provider span ownership, and OTel-enabled duplicate/privacy behavior are PR blockers. Detailed evidence is in 25-review.md and 25-attempts/.
